common.betaLanguage: This language is in beta. Safety messages may appear in English.

hero.title: Clinically informed guidance for your symptoms.
hero.subtitle: Free. Anonymous. Describe your symptoms and get a doctor-ready health report.
hero.placeholder: Describe your symptom, e.g. "I've had a headache since yesterday"
hero.privacy: No account needed to start. Your conversation is private.
hero.placeholderLong: Describe symptom or ask any health questions

chat.redirect: Let's stay focused on your health question so I can help you.

demographics.prompt: To give you safe, relevant guidance, I need two details.

reportGate.title: Your report is ready
reportGate.body: Sign in with Google to save this report and your history, or continue as a guest.
reportGate.skipNote: Guest reports are available for 7 days and are not saved to an account.

safety.disclaimer: LibertyMD is an informational service and not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Your conversation is safe.
errors.offline: You appear to be offline. Reconnect to continue.
errors.retryMessage: Tap to retry your last message.

app.howItWorksSubtitle: Simple enough to use when you feel unwell.
app.handoffTitle: Continue with a doctor when you need one.
app.doctorReview: Can review the AI report and advise next steps for {price}.
app.humanCareSubtitle: Every triage is designed to hand off cleanly to a licensed physician.

steps.title: Share your symptoms
steps.description: Tell LibertyMD what you feel, when it began, and what worries you most. You do not need to know the medical words.

steps.title: Focussed Follow-up
steps.description: LibertyMD asks concise questions about timing, severity, history, and context, adapting each follow-up to your answers.

steps.title: Safety Screen
steps.description: Clinical guardrails look for warning signs throughout the conversation and clearly explain when urgent or emergency care is needed.

steps.title: Doctor Ready Report
steps.description: Your answers become a structured summary with the symptom timeline, safety guidance, next steps, and a doctor-ready SOAP note.

care.title: Urgent and primary care, without starting over.
care.body: Your complete intake moves with you, so a physician already has the symptom history and context. Less time repeating yourself. More time focused on care.
care.priceLead: Video visits with licensed doctors for

pricing.title: Care that stays clear about cost.
pricing.body: Start with a free private chat. Continue to a licensed online doctor only when you choose to.

stories.title: A clearer conversation can change what happens next.
stories.body: Sample patient stories showing how a structured health conversation can bring clarity before the next step.

items.title: I arrived knowing what to explain.
items.quote: LibertyMD organized the timeline and warning signs, so my appointment started with the details that mattered.

items.title: The next step finally felt clear.
items.quote: The conversation separated what I could monitor at home from the symptoms that would need urgent care.

items.title: I had better questions for my doctor.
items.quote: Instead of searching through dozens of pages, I left with a concise report and a focused list of questions.

items.title: It made the warning signs easy to understand.
items.quote: The safety questions were calm and direct. I understood why same-day care mattered and what to tell the clinician.

items.title: I could pick up without starting over.
items.quote: My timeline, medications, and next steps stayed together, which made the follow-up conversation much less scattered.

items.title: I knew what to watch overnight.
items.quote: The plan gave me practical checkpoints for hydration, temperature, and the changes that would mean getting help.

items.title: The conversation made my options simpler.
items.quote: I could explain what I had already tried and understand which questions to take to my pharmacist and doctor.

items.title: I stopped guessing about the swelling.
items.quote: LibertyMD helped me describe the injury clearly and understand when an examination or imaging could be appropriate.

items.title: I felt calmer making the decision.
items.quote: The questions helped me organize what had changed and decide what kind of care my mother needed next.

items.title: The pattern finally became visible.
items.quote: Seeing the timing and triggers together gave me a much better starting point for a longer-term conversation.

items.title: I could think before choosing the next step.
items.quote: The summary gave me language for my concerns without making the situation feel more frightening than it was.

items.title: Nothing important slipped through the cracks.
items.quote: I had one place for the symptom changes, questions, and next steps I wanted to cover at my follow-up.

items.title: When a fever needs more than rest
items.description: A practical guide to duration, hydration, warning signs, and when to seek an evaluation.

items.title: Chest discomfort: the details that matter
items.description: Learn how timing, exertion, breathing, and associated symptoms change the urgency of care.

items.title: How to build a useful symptom timeline
items.description: Turn scattered observations into a concise story that is easier for a clinician to assess.

careControls.mergeNotice: That Google account already has LibertyMD history. Sign in to merge this consultation safely.
careControls.privateDefault: Private by default. Guest reports expire after seven days.
careControls.emptyHistory: Your completed consultations will appear here.

chatx.phDemo: Add age and sex above to continue
chatx.phReportGate: Choose how to view your report
chatx.phEnded: This consultation has ended for safety
